edges.description: Impaired PISD function disrupts phosphatidylethanolamine biosynthesis within mitochondria.
Mitochondrial structural integrity is compromised downstream of PISD dysfunction.
Loss of mitochondrial phosphatidylethanolamine homeostasis compromises mitochondrial membrane structure and fragmentation control.
PISD-related phospholipid metabolism disruption is linked to abnormal bone formation and skeletal dysplasia.
The PISD-related bone-formation branch includes disproportionate short stature within the spondyloepimetaphyseal skeletal phenotype.
The skeletal and connective-tissue branch includes severe joint laxity alongside skeletal dysplasia.
The skeletal dysplasia branch includes spinal curvature as part of the Liberfarb skeletal phenotype.
The same PISD phospholipid-metabolism mechanism is linked to sensory defects, including hearing impairment.
PISD-related phospholipid metabolism disruption is linked to the ocular sensory branch of the syndrome.
The ocular sensory branch can include optic disc pallor and optic atrophy in affected individuals.
PISD-related phospholipid metabolism disruption is linked to abnormal cerebral development.
PISD-related phospholipid metabolism disruption is linked to abnormal cerebral development and neurodevelopmental impairment.

nodes.description: Patient-derived cells show abnormal mitochondrial fragmentation, supporting a mitochondrial structural defect downstream of PISD dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Novel biallelic PISD missense variants cause spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia with disproportionate short stature and fragmented mitochondrial morphology
evidence.snippet: However, patient cells had a significantly higher proportion of fragmented mitochondria compared to control cells

nodes.description: Hearing loss is part of the multisystem Liberfarb syndrome phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: The Liberfarb syndrome, a multisystem disorder affecting eye, ear, bone, and brain development, is caused by a founder pathogenic variant in thePISD gene.
evidence.snippet: We observed four individuals in two unrelated but consanguineous families from Portugal and Brazil affected by early-onset retinal degeneration, sensorineural hearing loss, microcephaly, intellectual disability, and skeletal dysplasia with scoliosis and short stature.

nodes.description: Defective PISD activity impairs conversion of phosphatidylserine to phosphatidylethanolamine, disrupting mitochondrial membrane homeostasis.

evidence.reference_title: Novel biallelic PISD missense variants cause spondyloepimetaphyseal dysplasia with disproportionate short stature and fragmented mitochondrial morphology
evidence.snippet: The PISD precursor is self-cleaved to generate a heteromeric mature enzyme that converts phosphatidylserine to the phospholipid phosphatidylethanolamine.

evidence.reference_title: The Liberfarb syndrome, a multisystem disorder affecting eye, ear, bone, and brain development, is caused by a founder pathogenic variant in thePISD gene.
evidence.snippet: PISD is required to maintain mitochondrial PE (mtPE), as PE imported from the CDP-ethanolamine pathway alone cannot sustain adequate mtPE levels.
